hooks.prompt:
A bash command was just run. Input: $ARGUMENTS

Only act if ALL of these are true:
1. The command contains git push
2. The command targets the main branch
3. The tool_response does not contain error or rejected (push succeeded)

If conditions are NOT met, stop immediately and do nothing.

If conditions ARE met, perform ALL steps below:

STEP 1 - Gather context:
Run: git log --oneline -8
Run: git show --stat HEAD

STEP 2 - Update /home/user/designMD/ROADMAP.md:
a) Change the Last updated line near the top to today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format.
b) In the ## Done (recent) section at the bottom, prepend a new bullet in this format:
   - [x] **YYYY-MM-DD** - <one-sentence summary of what shipped> (commit-sha first 7 chars)
   Use the actual commit SHA and describe what changed from the git log.
c) If a commit clearly closes a roadmap item (P1-x, P2-x, B-x), also mark it [x] in its phase section.
d) Do NOT rewrite or delete existing Done entries.

STEP 3 - Update memory at ~/.claude/projects/-home-user-designMD/memory/:
a) Create the directory if it does not exist.
b) Read the existing file deployments.md if it exists (or create it fresh).
c) Prepend a new section at the top of deployments.md:
   ## YYYY-MM-DD - <short summary>
   - Commits: <comma-separated short SHAs>
   - Changed: <key files changed>
   - Status: deployed to production (uiuxskills.com)
d) Keep all previous entries below - never delete old entries.

Be concise. Do not produce any chat output - just perform the file writes silently.
